metadata.title: Official-source enrichment profiles for selected Indian credit cards
metadata.method: Issuer product pages and issuer catalogues were fetched on the checked date. Product-page statements were retained as exact evidence excerpts by field; absent facts are explicit unknowns. Linked official PDFs/terms were recorded as sources but not treated as supporting a field unless the product page excerpt supported it.
important_limitations: Evidence excerpts are contractual-source candidates, not editorial estimates. Conflicting/current-vs-legacy statements can coexist and require human review before rules-engine publication.
Unknown means not found on the inspected official surfaces, not that the benefit, fee, or criterion does not exist.
SBI annual fees were captured from issuer-rendered catalogue tiles. Joining fees were not inferred from annual fees.
Eligibility is captured only for explicit age, income, invite-only, or select-customer wording; generic benefit eligibility is excluded.
Taxes are not included unless the excerpt states otherwise.

field_semantics.normalized: Machine-readable value only where directly supported without inference; otherwise null.
field_semantics.evidence: Exact official-page excerpt, source URL, and checked date.

What is the fuel surcharge waiver offered to Cardholders?

Cardholders can avail of a 1% fuel surcharge waiver on all fuel transactions of up to ₹4,000 at any fuel outlet in India. This benefit applies to both the primary Cardholder and the Plus (supplementary) cardholder, helping them save on regular fuel expenses. The waiver is automatically applied when the eligible transaction is completed using the Credit Card.

Note that the insurance covers international travel only, provided that the tickets were purchased using the Credit Card. Supplementary Cardholders are not eligible for this insurance coverage. You can earn 2% ICICI Bank reward points on all eligible domestic transactions. However, there is a cap of 5,000 reward points per statement cycle for the following categories: Utility payments Insurance premium payments Education payments Government payments (such as property tax, advance tax, fees, court payments, postal payments, etc.) Additionally, ICICI Bank reward points will not be earned on fuel transactions, rent payments or transactions converted into EMIs. The points earned on such transactions will be reversed.
